reasoning: This is an anomaly detection question looking for a 10-fold increase in fatalities relative to the 360-day average. While the prompt mentions the most recent value was '1.000', this is a preliminary or buggy indicator (either meaning 1,000 monthly average or an artifact 1.0 returned from missing data). The criteria clearly state the baseline will be recalculated from the finally published ACLED dataset. Given that ACLED regularly records 500 to over 1,000 fatalities per month in Somalia due to the ongoing conflict with Al Shabaab, the true historically adjusted baseline is roughly 1,000 fatalities. A 10x event would therefore demand 5,000 to 10,000 fatalities within a single 30-day window. Such extreme numbers usually only correspond to sudden onsets of massive international wars or genocides (e.g. initial Ukraine invasion or Gaza conflict peaks), which are highly implausible for the Somali theater by early 2026. The 2025-11-16 window encompasses the current present without any such massive spike occurring, so its probability sits at the absolute minimum. Uncertainty slightly increases for further horizon dates, but remains very low.
